_description: Python CLI and library anti-patterns — practitioner-informed rules from Dagster Engineering, Seth Larson, Trey Hunner, Hynek Schlawack, Brett Cannon, Glyph Lefkowitz, and Ned Batchelder

rules.message: [architecture] Use pathlib.Path instead of os.path for modern Python
[observability] Bare print() in library code — use logging module or click.echo for CLIs
[security] Use subprocess.run() instead of .call()/.Popen() — better error handling and defaults
[architecture] Use X | None instead of Optional[X] (Python 3.10+)
[observability] Swallowed exception — log the error or handle it explicitly, never silently pass
[security] shell=True enables shell injection — use list arguments instead of shell strings
[security] Insecure deserialization — pickle.load is arbitrary code execution, yaml.load needs Loader=SafeLoader
[security] re.match() only checks the start of the string — use re.fullmatch() for input validation
[architecture] Don't inherit from dict/list — use UserDict/UserList or composition instead
[architecture] Untyped dict — use TypedDict, dataclass, or msgspec/pydantic model for structured data
rules._source: Brett Cannon (snarky.ca), Dagster Engineering (dagster.io/blog), Hilmar Gustafsson (hilmargustafs.com)

rules.message: [security] os.path.commonprefix is a string operation, not a path operation — use os.path.commonpath() instead
[architecture] Unverified typing.cast — add assert isinstance() before cast to validate at runtime
[testability] Meaningless assertion — assert True/1 always passes, test actual behavior instead
